meaning: n.大地穴(美国西部和墨西哥等地印第安人用作会堂的一种建筑)
examples: Under this theory, Christian domination over indigenous faiths is dramatized by surrounding the kiva with Christian buildings.
For example, the mission churches share certain spatial qualities with the indigenous kiva, a round, partly subterranean room used by many Southwest Native American communities for important rituals.
In deference to European custom, the ceilings of these churches were higher than those of the traditional kiva.
Kiva is a developing world micro-finance organisation with a difference: it is based in San Francisco.
He hopes to provide steady work to 100 people in Thailand through their new partnership with Kiva.
